hypothesis: If a multimodal contrastive model is trained to align SMILES strings with 3D electron density voxel grids, then the learned molecular embeddings will be emergently invariant to arbitrary 3D rotations—yielding higher rotated-molecule retrieval accuracy, greater pose-diverse retrieval rates, and improved property-clustering and linear-probe prediction compared to unimodal 3D or SMILES baselines—because cross-modal contrastive alignment encourages extraction of rotation-agnostic chemical semantics shared between sequence and density modalities.
